meta.title: NAAC Audio Sync Debrief
meta.description: A compact debrief comparing the local drone checkout, the M3 naac checkout, Google Drive audio visibility, DVC materialization, and the repeatable setup decision.

props.title: Audio Sync Debrief
props.subtitle: The M3 audit did not find the same world as this drone checkout. Treat that as signal: repo-local audio, DVC materialization, and Google Drive sync are currently three different surfaces.
props.meta: Generated 2026-06-13 from M3 audit attachment plus live /production/drone refresh

props.content: The immediate decision is not model training. It is to standardize the audio root contract: keep DVC-managed repo materialization separate from Google Drive dataset sync, and pass explicit `--audio-root` values until a canonical Drive root is selected and verified.

props.label: Snapshot
props.title: What changed after reading the M3 audit

props.label: M3 repo MP3s across LiveATC classes
Verdict
props.title: Do not treat these machines as synced yet
M3 audit result
props.content: M3 audited `/production/naac`, not `/production/drone`. It did not find a usable NAAC Google Drive audio root. Its `data/audio` is a normal directory with large local/DVC materialization.

props.title: This machine result
props.content: The current `/production/drone` checkout also has normal repo-local `data/audio`, but Google Drive Desktop does expose `Clients/NAAC/Data/audio` and a shortcut-target LiveATC archive path.

rows.meaning: The M3 agent did not audit the requested path because it was missing there.
M3 may be stale relative to current drone work.
Neither repo is wired to Drive through `data/audio`.
Git tracks DVC metadata, not raw audio payloads.

rows.m3: class-b, class-c, class-d, class-d-towered, class-g-ctaf, jobs, logs
rows.meaning: M3 has much more local audio than this checkout.

rows.m3: No usable NAAC audio root found; argomarine Drive provider stalled
rows.drone: Shared Drive path exists; shortcut-target LiveATC archives exist
rows.meaning: Drive account/root setup differs by machine.

props.label: Inventory
props.title: Audio counts and roots worth keeping straight

rows.contents: All top-level datasets plus LiveATC archive classes; 50,999 MP3s reported
rows.action: Use explicit repo-local `--audio-root` on M3 until Drive root is fixed
Do not infer this is a full training corpus

rows.contents: ATCO2, atc_asr_dataset, atcosim, liveatc, uwb_atcc; about 13G locally visible
rows.action: Prefer this as the human-readable canonical root if archives are moved or exposed here

rows.contents: class-b, class-c, class-d, class-d-towered, class-g-ctaf, jobs, logs
rows.authority: Currently exposes useful archive tree, but path is opaque
rows.action: Do not make this the long-term documented root unless folder ID is captured

props.label: Setup Model
props.title: Repeatable audio setup should separate four concerns
1. Source of files
props.content: Google Drive Shared Drive or local DVC materialization. This is where bytes live.

props.title: 2. Local resolver
props.content: `NAAC_AUDIO_ROOT` or explicit `--audio-root`. This is what tools read.

props.title: 3. Optional symlink
props.content: `data/audio` can become an uncommitted symlink only after the canonical Drive root is chosen.

props.title: 4. Verifier
props.content: A small audit command should prove root exists, classes exist, counts are plausible, and stale absolute paths are flagged.

rows: M3 has existing DVC/materialized audio and this machine has two competing Drive roots. A symlink now would hide the mismatch.
Pass `--audio-root` or set `NAAC_AUDIO_ROOT` per machine. This keeps setup reversible.
The human-readable `Clients/NAAC/Data/audio` path is easier to document than `.shortcut-targets-by-id`.
M3 found no usable NAAC Drive root and appears to be on `argomarine.ai` Drive provider.
Need the exact iter4 data source, training method, audio treatment, and where Sunny's audio is synced.

props.content:
repo=/Users/jean-patricksmith/digital/kingly/apps/production/drone
cd "$repo"

# repo-local materialization
git -C data ls-files 'audio/liveatc/archives/*.dvc'
stat -f '%N | type=%HT | link-target=%Y' data/audio data/audio/liveatc data/audio/liveatc/archives
find data/audio/liveatc/archives -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -exec basename {} \; | sort

# Drive candidates
find "$HOME/Library/CloudStorage" -maxdepth 5 -type d -iname '*audio*' 2>/dev/null | sort

# Current explicit source-root style
uv run --extra liveatc liveatc-train \
  --audio-root /ABSOLUTE/PATH/TO/audio/liveatc \
  --out-root data/training/liveatc \
  --profile fast-local

props.label: JP machine
props.title: Choose canonical Drive root
props.content: Decide whether `Clients/NAAC/Data/audio` becomes canonical and move/expose LiveATC archives there, or document the shortcut-target folder ID explicitly.

props.label: M3
props.title: Repair Drive visibility
props.content: M3 should install/sign into the correct Google Drive account, verify Shared Drive access, and rerun the same audit against `/production/drone` if that checkout exists.

props.label: Repo
props.title: Add onboarding verifier
props.content: Document `NAAC_AUDIO_ROOT`, explicit `--audio-root`, DVC materialization, and a no-destructive audit command. Do not create a symlink until root choice is final.

props.label: Iter5
props.title: Keep training gated
props.content: The iter5 exporter plan should consume explicit audio roots and provenance. It should not infer corpus authority from whichever local folder happens to be hydrated.
